comment_text.sn44.7:1.1: See SN 33.1:1.2 and note for Vacchagotta. | This discourse introduces the full set of ten undeclared points.
comment_text.sn44.7:2.1: Loka originally meant “light”, hence the sky bright with gods (stars), from which developed the meanings “heaven”, a “world”, and the “cosmos”. Sometimes Vedists believed that an “eternal world” was won by those worthy (DN 1:1.31.5). However, the Suttas often discuss the Upaniṣadic notion that the “self and the cosmos are eternal” (DN 1:1.30.1, MN 102:14.2), where loka means the “cosmos” as a whole, not a specific heavenly “world”. But the distinction is not always clear-cut. See also next note.
comment_text.sn44.7:2.7: The distinction between a limited “world” and an infinite “cosmos” was said to depend on the wisdom and spiritual depth of the individual: “Those who venerate (water, breath, and moon) as finite win only a limited world, whereas those who venerate them as infinite win a world without limit” (Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 1.5.13, Olivelle translation).
comment_text.sn44.7:2.13: The term “soul” (jīva) was favored by the samaṇas, as opposed to the “self” (attā) of the brahmins. Both are rejected by the Buddha as forms of “metaphysical” self: they postulate the absolute, eternal existence of entities that cannot be established empirically. The repeated demonstrative pronouns (taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīraṁ) assert an emphatic identity.

root_text.sn44.7:1.1: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā mahāmoggallānena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.7:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.7:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, sassato loko”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.7: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, antavā loko”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.13: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.16: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.19: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.22: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.25: “Kiṁ nu kho, bho moggallāna, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.27: ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.7:2.28: “Kiṁ pana, bho moggallāna, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:2.30: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.7:3.1: “Ko nu kho, bho moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn44.7:3.2: sassato lokoti vā, asassato lokoti vā, antavā lokoti vā, anantavā lokoti vā, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti vā, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīranti vā, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā?
root_text.sn44.7:3.3: Ko pana, bho moggallāna, hetu ko paccayo, yena samaṇassa gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn44.7:3.4: sassato lokotipi, asassato lokotipi, antavā lokotipi, anantavā lokotipi, taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīrantipi, aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīrantipi, hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipi, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipī”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:4.1: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā cakkhuṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:4.2: jivhaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:4.3: manaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti.
root_text.sn44.7:4.4: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn44.7:4.6: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā.
root_text.sn44.7:4.7: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho cakkhuṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:4.8: jivhaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:4.9: manaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati.
root_text.sn44.7:4.10: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti—
root_text.sn44.7:4.12: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇātipī”ti.
root_text.sn44.7:5.1: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn44.7:5.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn44.7:5.3: “kiṁ nu kho, bho gotama, sassato loko”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:5.5: “Kiṁ pana, bho gotama, neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:5.7: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti.
root_text.sn44.7:6.1: “Ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo, yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.7:6.3: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā?
root_text.sn44.7:6.4: Ko pana, bho gotama, hetu ko paccayo, yena bhoto gotamassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.7:6.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti?
root_text.sn44.7:7.1: “Aññatitthiyā kho, vaccha, paribbājakā cakkhuṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:7.2: jivhaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:7.3: manaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassanti.
root_text.sn44.7:7.4: Tasmā aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ puṭṭhānaṁ evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.7:7.6: ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’ti vā.
root_text.sn44.7:7.7: Tathāgato ca kho, vaccha, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho cakkhuṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:7.8: jivhaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati …pe…
root_text.sn44.7:7.9: manaṁ ‘netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na meso attā’ti samanupassati.
root_text.sn44.7:7.10: Tasmā tathāgatassa evaṁ puṭṭhassa na evaṁ veyyākaraṇaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn44.7:7.11: ‘sassato loko’tipi, ‘asassato loko’tipi, ‘antavā loko’tipi, ‘anantavā loko’tipi, ‘taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘aññaṁ jīvaṁ aññaṁ sarīran’tipi, ‘hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘hoti ca na ca hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipi, ‘neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’tipī”ti.
root_text.sn44.7:8.2: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmiṁ.
root_text.sn44.7:8.3: Idānāhaṁ, bho gotama, samaṇaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ apucchiṁ.
root_text.sn44.7:8.4: Samaṇopi me moggallāno etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi tamatthaṁ byākāsi, seyyathāpi bhavaṁ gotamo.
root_text.sn44.7:8.5: Acchariyaṁ, bho gotama, abbhutaṁ, bho gotama.
root_text.sn44.7:8.6: Yatra hi nāma satthu ca sāvakassa ca atthena attho byañjanena byañjanaṁ saṁsandissati samessati na virodhayissati, yadidaṁ aggapadasmin”ti.

translation_text.sn44.7:1.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.7:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to Mahāmoggallāna:
translation_text.sn44.7:2.1: “Worthy Moggallāna, is this right: ‘the cosmos is eternal’?”
translation_text.sn44.7:2.2: “Vaccha, this has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.7:2.4: “Then is this right: ‘the cosmos is not eternal’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.13: ‘the soul and the body are one and the same’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.16: ‘the soul is one thing, the body another’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.19: ‘a realized one still exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.22: ‘a realized one no longer exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.25: ‘a realized one both still exists and no longer exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn44.7:2.28: ‘a realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn44.7:2.29: “This too has not been declared by the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn44.7:3.1: “What’s the cause, worthy Moggallāna, what’s the reason why, when the wanderers of other religions are asked these questions, they declare one of these to be true?
translation_text.sn44.7:3.3: And what’s the reason why, when the ascetic Gotama is asked these questions, he does not declare one of these to be true?”
translation_text.sn44.7:4.1: “Vaccha, the wanderers of other religions regard the eye like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’ They regard the ear … nose …
translation_text.sn44.7:4.3: mind like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’
translation_text.sn44.7:4.4: That’s why, when asked, they declare one of those answers to be true.
translation_text.sn44.7:4.7: The Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha regards the eye like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’ He regards the ear … nose …
translation_text.sn44.7:4.9: mind like this: ‘This is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self.’
translation_text.sn44.7:4.10: That’s why, when asked, he does not declare one of those answers to be true.”
translation_text.sn44.7:5.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta got up from his seat and went to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn44.7:5.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side. He asked the Buddha the same questions, and received the same answers.
translation_text.sn44.7:8.1: He said, “It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing!
translation_text.sn44.7:8.2: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!
translation_text.sn44.7:8.3: Just now I went to the ascetic Mahāmoggallāna and asked him about this matter.
translation_text.sn44.7:8.4: And he explained it to me with these words and phrases, just like the worthy Gotama.
translation_text.sn44.7:8.5: It’s incredible, worthy Gotama, it’s amazing!
translation_text.sn44.7:8.6: How the meaning and the phrasing of the teacher and the disciple fit together and agree without contradiction when it comes to the chief matter!”
